subject: Code Smell Scan —SquadDash Full Codebase
body:
## Code Smell Scan — SquadDash Full Codebase

**Branch:** `maintenance/20260525-code-smells`  
**Scope:** All C# source files (`SquadDash/`, `SquadDashLauncher/`, `VoiceHeuristics/`) and TypeScript SDK (`Squad.SDK/`)  
**Total findings:** ~103 across5 scan batches

---

## C# — Core Services

| # | File | Anchor | Category | Issue | Fix |
|---|------|--------|----------|-------|-----|
| 1 | `SquadDash/AgentStatusCard.cs` | Class | Static State Inconsistency | Static fields `_isDarkTheme`, `_imagesVisible`, `_liveInstances` manage app-wide state inside an instance class; UI desync possible | Extract into a dedicated static service class with event notification |
| 2 | `SquadDash/AgentThreadIdentityPolicy.cs` | `NormalizeKey` | Duplicated Logic | Near-identical to `BackgroundAgentLaunchInfoResolver.NormalizeKey`; both strip non-alphanumeric chars and lowercase | Extract to shared utility |
| 3 | `SquadDash/AgentThreadRegistry.cs` | Constructor | Long Parameter List | 18 action-delegate parameters; very hard to instantiate and test | Group delegates into configuration objects or use a factory |
| 4 | `SquadDash/ApplicationSettingsStore.cs` | All `Save*` methods | Duplicated Logic | ~40 public `Save*` methods all follow the exact same acquire-load-modify-save-return pattern | Create a private generic helper `SaveSetting<T>(Func<…> updater)` |
| 5 | `SquadDash/BackgroundAgentLaunchInfoResolver.cs` | `ScoreCandidate` | Magic Numbers | Hard-coded score values 120/110/100/90/70/60 with no explanation of their meaning | Define as named constants with scoring strategy doc |
| 6 | `SquadDash/BackgroundAgentLaunchInfoResolver.cs` | `TryExtractAgentPrefix` | Duplicated Logic | String-splitting on `'-','_','.',space` repeated across multiple methods | Extract to shared segmentation helper |
| 7 | `SquadDash/BackgroundCancelCompletionPolicy.cs` | `ShouldForceFinalize` | Unnecessary Abstraction | Entire class wraps a single boolean with no added logic (`return cancelAcknowledged;`) | Inline into callers or add a doc comment explaining the abstraction |
| 8 | `SquadDash/BackgroundTaskPresenter.cs` | Constructor | Long Parameter List | 13 constructor parameters | Group into a configuration object |
| 9 | `SquadDash/BackgroundTaskPresenter.cs` | `BuildBackgroundTaskReport` | Magic Numbers | `Take(2)` and `Take(4)` without explanation | Define `MaxDisplayedAgents = 2` etc. |
| 10 | `SquadDash/CommitApprovalStore.cs` | `Load` | Inefficient Pattern | Re-orders and truncates the entire list on every load, even when unchanged | Only truncate/order when `Count > MaxItems` |
| 11 | `SquadDash/DocStatusStore.cs` | `GetKey` | Inconsistent Path Normalization | Normalizes to forward-slashes but not to a consistent case, despite the dictionary using `OrdinalIgnoreCase` | Also call `.ToLowerInvariant()` in `GetKey` |
| 12 | `SquadDash/DocTopicsLoader.cs` | `TitleCase` | Inefficient Pattern | Uses `w.Substring(1)` (deprecated) instead of modern `w[1..]` | Use C# range syntax |
| 13 | `SquadDash/DocTopicsLoader.cs` | `ExtractMarkdownTitle` | Bare Catch | All exceptions swallowed silently; file access issues invisible | Log exception or narrow catch to expected types |
| 14 | `SquadDash/HostCommandParser.cs` | `ParseInvocation` | Missing Null Check | `commandProp.GetString()` can return null; used without check at `.Trim()` | Add `?.Trim()` and null guard |
| 15 | `SquadDash/HostCommandRegistry.cs` | `TryParseExtensionParameter` | Duplicated Logic | Null-checking pattern for `JsonElement` properties repeated4+ times across methods | Extract a helper `TryGetString(JsonElement, string, out string)` |
| 16 | `SquadDash/IdleDetectionService.cs` | `Start` | Magic Numbers | `50`, `30_000`, and divisor `3` lack explanation | Define `MinIntervalMs`, `MaxIntervalMs`, `IntervalDivisor` constants |
| 17 | `SquadDash/InboxMessageParser.cs` | `TryExtract` | Complex Conditional | Brace-depth + escape-state tracking loop is dense and hard to follow | Extract brace-matching into a named `FindClosingBrace` helper |
| 18 | `SquadDash/InboxPanelController.cs` | `BuildAttachmentChip` | Long Method | 113-line method handling 5attachment types with duplicated error-handling | Extract each `case` into `BuildUrlAttachment`, `BuildFileAttachment`, etc. |
| 19 | `SquadDash/InboxPanelController.cs` | `BuildAttachmentChip` | Bare Catch (×5) | Five empty `catch {}` blocks swallow all exceptions | Log or narrow to expected exception types |
| 20 | `SquadDash/InboxStore.cs` | `NormalizeSubject` | Uncached Regex | Two regexes compiled inline on every call; unicode chars not explained | Cache as `static readonly` fields with comments |
| 21 | `SquadDash/InboxStore.cs` | `Save` | Inefficient Pattern | Always deserializes and re-serializes subject even when normalization produces same value | Normalize first; only replace if changed |
| 22 | `SquadDash/JsonFileStorage.cs` | `AtomicWrite` (both overloads) | Duplicated Logic | Two overloads share nearly identical temp-file and file-replacement logic | Extract shared logic into a private helper |
| 23 | `SquadDash/JsonFileStorage.cs` | `AtomicWrite` | Bare Catch | Empty `catch` on `File.Replace` swallows `IOException` | Catch specific type and log or rethrow |

---

## C# — Prompt, Transcript & Loop

| # | File | Anchor | Category | Issue | Fix |
|---|------|--------|----------|-------|-----|
| 24 | `SquadDash/LoopController.cs` | `RunLoopAsync` | Swallowed Exception | Bare `catch { }` silently discards all exceptions | At minimum log/trace |
| 25 | `SquadDash/LoopController.cs` | `DetectBuildCommand` | Repeated String Literals | File extensions `.sln`, `.csproj`, `.toml`, `go.mod` hardcoded without central definition | Create constants or a static list |
| 26 | `SquadDash/LoopMdParser.cs` | `Parse` | Magic Numbers | Indent levels 2/4/5 for YAML parsing with no names | Define `IndentMainOption`, `IndentSubOption` constants |
| 27 | `SquadDash/MaintenanceMdParser.cs` | `Parse` | Magic Numbers | Indent values 6and 4 hardcoded throughout | Extract to named YAML indent-level constants |
| 28 | `SquadDash/MaintenanceRunner.cs` | `BuildPrompt` / `ApplySafetyFloor` | Duplicate String Literals | Safety level strings `"report-only"`, `"branch"`, `"direct"` repeated across methods/switches | Create a `SafetyLevel` enum or static constants class |
| 29 | `SquadDash/MarkdownFlowDocumentBuilder.cs` | `AddTextWithCircleEmoji` | Inefficient Pattern | O(n×m) loop: iterates all emoji keys per character | Build a combined regex or trie; match once per string |
| 30 | `SquadDash/MarkdownFlowDocumentBuilder.cs` | `BoldItalicRegex` / `AddFormattedRuns` | Dead Code | Regex field defined but never used; `AddFormattedRuns` never called from text-processing path | Remove or wire up |
| 31 | `SquadDash/MarkdownHtmlBuilder.cs` | `Build` | Long Parameter List | Multiple optionalternary parameters make the method hard to test | Extract color resolution into a configuration object |
| 32 | `SquadDash/NotesStore.cs` | `WriteContent` | Inefficient Instantiation | Creates `new UTF8Encoding(false)` inline; BCL already provides `Encoding.UTF8` | Replace with `Encoding.UTF8` |
| 33 | `SquadDash/PromptInteractionLogic.cs` | `LocalPromptSubmissionPolicy` | Magic Strings | Slash command strings like `"/activate"` duplicated across `BusySafeCommands` and `ImmediateLocalCommands` | Define as `private static readonly` constants |
| 34 | `SquadDash/PromptQueue.cs` | `RenumberSequentially` | Minor Formatting | Missing space: `(){` instead of `() {` | Reformat|
| 35 | `SquadDash/PushToTalkController.cs` | `StartPushToTalkAsync` | Overly Broad Catch | `catch (Exception ex)` catches everything | Narrow to expected types |
| 36 | `SquadDash/PushToTalkController.cs` | `PttMaxTapHoldMs` | Magic Number | Value `250` unexplained | Add comment: `// UI gesture timing threshold (ms)` |
| 37 | `SquadDash/PushToTalkController.cs` | `AppendSpeechToPrompt` | Inefficient Pattern | Two separate `_promptTextBox.Text` assignments cause two UI updates | Combine into one |
| 38 | `SquadDash/QuickReplyContextPromptBuilder.cs` | `MaxContextChars` / `MaxFieldChars` | Magic Numbers | 12000 and 1800 with no rationale | Add comments explaining the limits |
| 39 | `SquadDash/TasksContextBuilder.cs` | `ParseOpenGroups` | Magic Number | Hardcoded `2` for closing `**` length | Define `const int BoldMarkerLength = 2` |
| 40 | `SquadDash/TasksPanelParser.cs` | `Parse` | Magic Number | Same `2` for `**` closing marker | Same fix|
| 41 | `SquadDash/TranscriptSelectionController.cs` | `GetThread1` | Null Coalescing Chain | Multi-level `??` chain obscures intent | Use explicit `if`/`else` |
| 42 | `SquadDash/TranscriptTextUtilities.cs` | `FindTopLevelInboxSentinelIndex` | Complex Logic | Fence-tracking line parser is dense; subtle offset arithmetic | Extract fence logic into a named helper |
| 43 | `SquadDash/TranscriptTextUtilities.cs` | `FormatThinkingText` | Unmaintainable Regex | Very long regex with many hardcoded word suffixes; hard to extend | Build pattern programmatically from a configurable list |
| 44 | `SquadDash/WorkspaceConversationStore.cs` | Class-level constants | Magic Numbers | `MaxTurns=200`, `MaxAgentThreads=80`, `MaxToolOutputLength=100_000` without rationale | Add XML doc comments explaining the limits |

---

## C# — UI, Utilities & Annotations

| # | File | Anchor | Category | Issue | Fix |
|---|------|--------|----------|-------|-----|
| 45 | `SquadDash/AnnotationCursors.cs` | Cursor properties | Repetitive Code | Same `??=` + `CreateCursorFromDrawing()` pattern 9times with only hotspot values varying | Consider a generic lazy-init factory|
| 46 | `SquadDash/App.xaml.cs` | `OnStartup` | Bare Catch | `catch { /* best-effort */ }` on diagnostics file append | Log to fallback or surface failure |
| 47 | `SquadDash/BulletedTextBox.cs` | `HandlePreviewTextInput` | Logic Error | Condition uses `\|\|` where `&&` is almost certainly intended: `!IsNullOrWhiteSpace(line)\|\| line.StartsWith(prefix)` will evaluate StartsWith on whitespace/null | Change to `&&` |
| 48 | `SquadDash/ClipboardImageEditorWindow.cs` | Constants | Magic Numbers | `HandleSize=9.0`, `HitPad=5.0`, `MinSize=24.0` with no explanation | Add sizing-rationale comments |
| 49 | `SquadDash/ColorUtilities.cs` | `AccentBrush` / `CreateAccentBrush` | Duplicate Code | Two methods with identical hex-to-SolidColorBrush logic | Remove one; make it call the other |
| 50 | `SquadDash/ColorUtilities.cs` | `HslToRgb` | Magic Numbers | `1.0/3` and `2.0/3` inline without names | Extract `HueThreshold1= 1.0/3.0` etc. |
| 51 | `SquadDash/CurrentTurnStatusPresentation.cs` | `SlowUpstreamAverageChunkCharThreshold` | Magic Number | Value `12` with no context | Add comment: `// ~3 tokens at ~4 chars/token` |
| 52 | `SquadDash/DiffHoverPopup.cs` | `ShowDiff` | Magic Numbers | `Color.FromArgb(26, 107, 174, 214)` and sibling for red tint; `~10% opacity` mentioned in comment but values not constants | Define `AddedLineTintAlpha = 26` etc. |
| 53 | `SquadDash/DocumentUtilities.cs` | `FrontMatterRegex` | Unnecessary Compile | `RegexOptions.Compiled` on a regex used once per document load; startup cost likely not justified | Remove `Compiled` flag or profile first |
| 54 | `SquadDash/InboxMessageWindow.cs` | `BuildAttachmentChip` | Bare Catch (×4) | Empty catches for URL/file/task-ref/text launch failures; user gets no feedback | Log or show brief error to user |
| 55 | `SquadDash/NativeMethods.cs` | `TryActivateProcessMainWindow` | Poor Formatting | Excessive whitespace before `{` on method signature line | Reformat |
| 56 | `SquadDash/OpenAiTtsProvider.cs` | `SpeakAsync` | Resource Leak | `MediaPlayer` created in `BeginInvoke` and never disposed; temp file may be deleted before playback ends | Store player as class field; dispose after playback |
| 57 | `SquadDash/PromptContextDiagnostics.cs` | `GetRiskBand` | Magic Numbers | 10+ unnamed numeric thresholds: `120_000`, `50_000`, `20_000`, `30`, `15`, `90`… | Extract all to named constants with doc comments |
| 58 | `SquadDash/PromptHistoryStore.cs` | `Save` | Inefficient Pattern | `.Skip()` + `.ToArray()` iterates the list twice | Use `.TakeLast(MaxEntries).ToArray()` |
| 59 | `SquadDash/PromptTraceMetrics.cs` | `FormatCharsPerSecond` / `FormatAverageChunkSize` | Magic Strings | `"n/a"` and format string `"0.0"` inline | Extract to named constants|
| 60 | `SquadDash/SearchHighlightAdorner.cs` | `OnRender` | Bare Catch | All render exceptions swallowed; unexpected failures invisible | Add conditional logging to distinguish expected vs. unexpected |
| 61 | `SquadDash/SquadBridgePromptBuilder.cs` | `HiringIntentRegexes` | Poor Naming | Regex arrays lack per-pattern comments explaining what each detects | Add inline comments: `// "hire" verb pattern`, `// UI context false-positive` etc. |
| 62 | `SquadDash/SquadBridgePromptBuilder.cs` | `BuildCustomUniverseContext` | Long Hardcoded String | 200+ char instruction string embedded inline | Move to a `private const string` or resource file |
| 63 | `SquadDash/SquadCliAdapter.cs` | `LaunchPowerShellCommandWindow` | Long Method | Complex multi-line string building with nestedternaries for messages | Extract message building to helper method |
| 64 | `SquadDash/SquadDashEnvironment.cs` | `Initialize` | Missing Null Check | `applicationRoot` not validated before `Path.Combine` and `File.Exists` | Add `ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(applicationRoot)` |

---

## C# — Maintenance, Revision, Routing & Remaining

| # | File | Anchor | Category | Issue | Fix |
|---|------|--------|----------|-------|-----|
| 65 | `SquadDash/InstanceActivationChannel.cs` | `DisposeAsync` | Swallowed Cancellation | `OperationCanceledException` caught silently without comment | Add comment clarifying this is expected teardown behavior |
| 66 | `SquadDash/MaintenanceReportWriter.cs` | `TryReadStubSidecar` | Bare Catch | `catch { return null; }` hides deserialization errors | Log the exception|
| 67 | `SquadDash/MaintenanceStateStore.cs` | `IsEligible` | Complex Conditional | Large switch with nested conditionals and fallthrough for frequency evaluation | Extract each frequency type into a strategy method |
| 68 | `SquadDash/MaintenanceStubStateStore.cs` | `Load` | Bare Catch | `catch { }` swallows all load exceptions | Log or narrow exception type |
| 69 | `SquadDash/MarkdownHoverPopup.cs` | `GenerateQrBitmap` | Bare Catch (×2) | `catch { /* overlay is cosmetic */ }` hides unexpected failures | Log or separate cosmetic-skip from unexpected errors |
| 70 | `SquadDash/PostedUiActionTracker.cs` | `MarkCompleted` | Poor Naming | Parameter named `long _` (discard) but the reason it exists is undocumented | Document why the parameter exists or remove it |
| 71 | `SquadDash/PreferencesWindow.cs` | Constructor | Long Method | Initializes71+ fields in one constructor body | Split into named `InitializeSpeechSettings()`, `InitializeLayoutSettings()`, etc. |
| 72 | `SquadDash/PushNotificationService.cs` | `ShouldSend` | Magic Numbers | Thresholds `3`, `5`, `1`, `10`, `24` (minutes/hours) inline | Define `RateLimitEventThreshold`, `HighRateThreshold` etc. |
| 73 | `SquadDash/PushNotificationService.cs` | `DefaultEventToggles` | Magic Strings | Event type strings like `"assistant_turn_complete"` scattered across code | Extract to an enum or static constants class |
| 74 | `SquadDash/PromptAttachmentViewerWindow.cs` | `SizeToImage` | Magic Numbers | `0.90`, `24` (ChromeW), `70` (ChromeH) | Extract to class-level named constants |
| 75 | `SquadDash/QuickReplyAgentLaunchPolicy.cs` | `MatchesExpectedAgent` | Complex Conditional | Repeated normalized-value checks create nested complexity | Extract common check into a local helper |
| 76 | `SquadDash/QuickReplyRoutePresentation.cs` | `BuildCaption` | Suspicious Logic | Early-return conditions make later conditional branches redundant/unreachable | Linearize the conditional flow |
| 77 | `SquadDash/RcStatusPanel.cs` | `GenerateQrBitmap` | Resource Leak | `MemoryStream` not inside a `using`; if `bitmap.EndInit()` throws, stream leaks | Wrap in `using` |
| 78 | `SquadDash/RcStatusPanel.cs` | Multiple UI lookups | Magic Strings | Resource keys `"PopupSurface"`, `"ActivePanelBorder"` etc. hardcoded | Create a `ThemeKeys` constants class |
| 79 | `SquadDash/RevisionPendingIndicator.cs` | Class constants | Magic Numbers | `80` (AnimationIntervalMs), `2.5` (InnerRadius), `5.5` (OuterRadius), `1.3` (StrokeWidth) | Group into a configuration record or add comments |
| 80 | `SquadDash/SessionWorkspace.cs` | `Create` | Temporal Coupling | Initialization relies on implicit property-ordering with no invariant validation | Add validation that returned object is consistent|
| 81 | `SquadDash/SmoothDictationHelper.cs` | `Apply` | Potential Infinite Loop | Regex replacement loop runs until no changes; malformed input could loop excessively | Add a max-iteration guard |
| 82 | `SquadDash/SoundNotificationService.cs` | `PlayImmediate` | Overly Broad Catch | `catch (Exception ex)` catches everything | Narrow to `IOException`, `InvalidOperationException`, etc. |
| 83 | `SquadDash/ToolTranscriptData.cs` | `TrimForLiveTranscript` | Missing Null Check | `normalized[..maxChars]` slice without bounds verification | Add explicit length guard |
| 84 | `SquadDash/ToolTranscriptEntry.cs` | `TranscriptThoughtBlockView` | Dead Code | Class defined twice (duplicate definition in same file) | Remove the duplicate |
| 85 | `SquadDash/ToolTranscriptFormatter.cs` | `BuildAgentTurnStartMarker` | Missing Null Check | `normalizedTaskLabel.TrimEnd(…)` called without null guard in all paths | Add null check |
| 86 | `SquadDash/ToolTranscriptFormatter.cs` | `HumanizeToolName` vs `GetToolEmojiByName` | Inconsistent Pattern | One method null-checks its parameter; the other does not | Unify null handling across both methods |

---

## TypeScript SDK (`Squad.SDK/`)

| # | File | Anchor | Category | Issue | Fix |
|---|------|--------|----------|-------|-----|
| 87 | `Squad.SDK/index.ts` | `emit()` | Hard-Coded Debug Output | `console.log()` call in production code; no log-level control | Use a configurable logger or redirect to structured JSON output |
| 88 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `getObjectValue` / `getStringValue` / `getNumberValue` / `getBooleanValue` / `getRawStringValue` | Code Duplication | Five near-identical type-checking wrappers | Create a single generic `getValue<T>(source, key, typeGuard)` |
| 89 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `getEventStringValue` / `getEventRawStringValue` / `getEventNumberValue` / `getEventBooleanValue` | Code Duplication | Four more parallel functions with identical try-property-then-fallback pattern | Consolidate into generic `getEventValue<T>(event, key, typeGuard)` |
| 90 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `agentInfoEqual` | Long Comparison Chain | 16+ explicit `===` checks to compare one object type | Use a `deepEqual` utility or generate from type definition |
| 91 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `shellInfoEqual` | Long Comparison Chain | Same as above for8-property shell info comparison | Same fix |
| 92 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `findPackageRoot` | Infinite Loop Risk | `while (true)` without depth counter; if `parent === directory` never triggers, loops forever | Add a max-depth counter |
| 93 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `findCopilotPackageRoot` | Duplicated Logic | Duplicates directory-traversal loop from `findPackageRoot` | Extract common traversal into a shared utility |
| 94 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `resolveJsonPointer` | Potential Infinite Recursion | Calls `collectSchemaConstValues` with no depth limit on recursive JSON pointer resolution | Add a max-depth parameter |
| 95 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `attachSessionListeners` | Long Method | ~330-line function registering 20+ event handlers | Extract each listener into a named method |
| 96 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `emitSubagentToolEvent` | Long Function | ~65-line callback generator handling multiple overlapping event types | Extract per-event handlers: `onToolStart`, `onToolProgress`, `onToolComplete`|
| 97 | `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` | `sessions` Map | Memory Leak Risk | No TTL or cleanup; sessions can accumulate indefinitely over long runtimes | Add expiration or use `WeakMap` |
| 98 | `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `emitSubagentLifecycle` / `emitSubagentTool` | Code Duplication | Two near-identical destructure-and-spread functions (also flagged by `DUP-008`/`DUP-009` comments in-code) | Merge into a single parameterized `emitSubagent()` |
| 99 | `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `buildRunHandlers` | Long Closure + Mutable State | ~165-line function capturing multiple mutable closure vars (`startedThinking`, `rcAccumulatedContent`, etc.) | Extract into a class with explicit state properties |
| 100 | `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `handleRunLoop` | Long Function | ~125-line async function with manual Promise handling and a custom `_squadStopRequested` flag set on the process object | Extract listeners into methods; use EventEmitter; store state externally |
| 101 | `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `main()` | Long Function | ~250-line main loop with a `for await` dispatcher and many type-check branches | Extract request-type handling into a dispatcher function |
| 102 | `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `startTunnel` (ngrok section) | Magic Numbers | Polling: `500`ms interval, `24` attempts hardcoded | Define `NGROK_POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 500`, `NGROK_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 24` |
| 103 | `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | `startTunnel` (cloudflared section) | Duplicated Logic | Cloudflare startup duplicates ngrok polling pattern with different mechanism | Extract common tunnel-polling logic |
| 104 | `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | Multiple call sites | Type Safety | 8+ uses of `as any` (lines 609, 657, 731, 748, 1242, 1625, 1630, 1635) bypass TypeScript safety | Create properly typed interfaces instead |
| 105 | `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` | Lines ~1461-1470 | Commented-Out Code | Block of commented-out BYOK diagnostic code | Remove or implement as a feature flag |
| 106 | `Squad.SDK/inboxPromptInjection.ts` | `InboxMessageInstruction` | Hard-Coded Prompt | 28-line template string with detailed instructions embedded; no validation of JSON block format | Load from external template file or validate at init |

---

## Priority Summary

| Priority | Category | Count | Notes |
|----------|----------|-------|-------|
| 🔴 High | **Logic Error** | 1 | `BulletedTextBox.HandlePreviewTextInput` — `\|\|` should be `&&`; functional bug|
| 🔴 High | **Resource Leak** | 2 | `OpenAiTtsProvider` MediaPlayer; `RcStatusPanel` MemoryStream |
| 🔴 High | **Infinite Loop / Recursion Risk** | 3 | `findPackageRoot`, `findCopilotPackageRoot`, `resolveJsonPointer`, `SmoothDictationHelper.Apply` |
| 🔴 High | **Dead Code (duplicate class)** | 1 | `ToolTranscriptEntry.TranscriptThoughtBlockView` defined twice |
| 🟠 Medium | **Bare Catch / Swallowed Exceptions** | ~18 | Across `JsonFileStorage`, `InboxPanelController`, `InboxMessageWindow`, `DocTopicsLoader`, `MarkdownHoverPopup`, `App.xaml`, `MaintenanceStubStateStore`, `MaintenanceReportWriter`, etc. |
| 🟠 Medium | **Long Methods / Functions** | ~10 | `ApplicationSettingsStore` Save* pattern; `attachSessionListeners`; `buildRunHandlers`; `main()`; `PreferencesWindow` constructor |
| 🟠 Medium | **Duplicated Logic** | ~12 | `NormalizeKey`, `AtomicWrite`, SDK getter families, tunnel polling, etc. |
| 🟡 Low | **Magic Numbers / Strings** | ~22 | Scoring values, indent levels, MIME types, thresholds, timing constants |
| 🟡 Low | **Missing Null Checks** | 4 | `HostCommandParser`, `SquadDashEnvironment`, `ToolTranscriptData`, `ToolTranscriptFormatter` |
| 🟡 Low | **Type Safety (`as any`)** | 8 | Throughout `runPrompt.ts` |

actions.label: Fix high-priority bugs
actions.prompt:
Arjun: during a code-smell scan on2026-05-25 (branch `maintenance/20260525-code-smells`), two high-priority bugs were found inSquadDash-public:

1. **Logic Error — `SquadDash/BulletedTextBox.cs`, method `HandlePreviewTextInput`**: The condition uses `||` but should almost certainly use `&&`. Current: `if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(lineText) || lineText.StartsWith(BulletPrefix, ...))`. Because of short-circuit evaluation, when `lineText` is null/whitespace the second operand still fires (or the overall condition is true when it should not be), meaning bullet-prefix logic can run on empty lines. Fix: change `||` to `&&`.

2. **Resource Leak — `SquadDash/OpenAiTtsProvider.cs`, method `SpeakAsync`**: A `MediaPlayer` is created inside a `BeginInvoke` lambda but never stored or disposed. The temp audio file may also be deleted before playback completes. Fix: store the player in a class-level field, subscribe to its `MediaEnded` event to delete the temp file and call `Close()` / `Dispose()` on it.

3. **Resource Leak — `SquadDash/RcStatusPanel.cs`, method `GenerateQrBitmap`**: A `MemoryStream` is created but not wrapped in a `using` block. If `bitmap.EndInit()` throws, the stream leaks. Fix: wrap the `MemoryStream` creation in a `using` statement.

4. **Duplicate class — `SquadDash/ToolTranscriptEntry.cs`**: The class `TranscriptThoughtBlockView` is defined twice in the same file. Remove the duplicate definition.

All fixes should be committed to branch `maintenance/20260525-code-smells`. Include Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> in each commit message.
actions.hint: Send Arjun the logic error + resource leaks to fix

actions.label: Fix bare catch blocks
actions.prompt:
Arjun: during a code-smell scan on 2026-05-25 (branch `maintenance/20260525-code-smells`), ~18 bare / swallowed exception catch blocks were found acrossSquadDash-public. These all silently hide failures. Please add at minimum a `SquadDashTrace.Exception(...)` (or equivalent existing trace call) to each one, or narrow the catch to a specific expected exception type where appropriate.

Files and locations:
- `SquadDash/JsonFileStorage.cs` — `AtomicWrite` (both overloads), `catch` on `File.Replace` (lines ~32and ~54)
- `SquadDash/InboxPanelController.cs` — `BuildAttachmentChip`, five `catch {}` blocks (one per attachment type case)
- `SquadDash/InboxMessageWindow.cs` — `BuildAttachmentChip`, four `catch {}` blocks (URL/file/task-ref/text cases)
- `SquadDash/DocTopicsLoader.cs` — `ExtractMarkdownTitle`, catch-all at end of method
- `SquadDash/MarkdownHoverPopup.cs` — `GenerateQrBitmap`, two catch blocks marked `/* overlay is cosmetic */`
- `SquadDash/App.xaml.cs` — `OnStartup`, `catch { /* best-effort */ }` on diagnostics file append
- `SquadDash/MaintenanceStubStateStore.cs` — `Load`, `catch { }`
- `SquadDash/MaintenanceReportWriter.cs` — `TryReadStubSidecar`, `catch { return null; }`
- `SquadDash/LoopController.cs` — `RunLoopAsync`, bare `catch { }` block
- `SquadDash/SearchHighlightAdorner.cs` — `OnRender`, catch with comment but no logging

For the cosmetic/best-effort ones (App.xaml, MarkdownHoverPopup, SearchHighlightAdorner) a `// Known: ...` comment plus a debug-level trace is sufficient. For the others (JsonFileStorage, InboxPanelController, InboxMessageWindow, DocTopicsLoader, MaintenanceStubStateStore, MaintenanceReportWriter, LoopController) add proper trace logging.

Commit to branch `maintenance/20260525-code-smells`. Include Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> in commit messages.
actions.hint: Send Arjun the bare-catch cleanup task

actions.label: Extract magic numbers/strings
actions.prompt:
Add a backlog task: Extract magic numbers and strings to named constants acrossSquadDash-public. This was identified during a code-smell scan on 2026-05-25. Key locations:

actions.label: Refactor long methods
actions.prompt:
Add a backlog task: Refactor long methods and functions identified in aSquadDash-public code-smell scan on 2026-05-25:
- `SquadDash/ApplicationSettingsStore.cs` — ~40 Save* methods repeat identical acquire-load-modify-save pattern; create a private generic helper `SaveSetting<T>(Func<…> updater)`
- `SquadDash/AgentThreadRegistry.cs` — Constructor has 18 action-delegate parameters; group into configuration objects
- `SquadDash/BackgroundTaskPresenter.cs` — Constructor has 13 parameters; use a configuration object
- `SquadDash/PreferencesWindow.cs` — Constructor initializes 71+ fields; split into named init methods
- `SquadDash/InboxPanelController.cs` `BuildAttachmentChip` —113 lines; extract each case into BuildUrlAttachment, BuildFileAttachment, etc.
- `Squad.SDK/squadService.ts` `attachSessionListeners` — ~330 lines with 20+ event handlers; extract each into named methods
- `Squad.SDK/runPrompt.ts` `buildRunHandlers` — ~165 lines capturing mutable closure state; extract into a class
